trade.summary: Strong bilateral trade with the Stati Uniti as Svezia's largest single-country export destination outside the EU. Svezia exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, vehicles, and defence equipment to the Stati Uniti; the Stati Uniti exports aircraft, machinery, chemicals, and software to Svezia. Swedish multinationals (Ericsson, Volvo, AstraZeneca) have major Stati Uniti operations.

diplomatic.summary: Svezia joined NATO in March 2024, deepening an already close partnership. The Stati Uniti was a strong advocate for Svezia's NATO accession. Bilateral relations are anchored in shared democratic values, liberal trade, and common security interests. Svezia aligns closely with Stati Uniti positions on Russia, Cina technology policy, and global governance. The relationship gained new strategic depth post-2022.

military.summary: Full NATO allies since March 2024. The Stati Uniti and Svezia have significantly expanded defence cooperation since Russia's invasion of Ucraina. Stati Uniti forces have access to Swedish bases. Svezia is acquiring advanced Stati Uniti systems including the Patriot air defence system and cooperates closely on Baltic Sea security. Joint exercises are extensive under NATO frameworks.

financial.summary: Deep financial ties. Swedish firms have massive Stati Uniti operations and Stati Uniti companies are major investors in Svezia. AP pension funds and the Riksbank hold large Stati UnitiD-denominated assets. Silicon Valley tech firms have major Svezia presences. Bilateral investment is among the largest in the transatlantic relationship per capita.

energy.summary: Growing energy cooperation. Svezia imports some Stati Uniti LNG as part of European gas diversification post-Ucraina war. Stati Uniti firms invest in Svezia's offshore wind sector. Cooperation on nuclear energy technology through Westinghouse and Svezia's nuclear programme.

scientific.summary: Among the world's deepest bilateral research relationships per capita. Joint work at leading institutions in biomedical sciences, physics, climate research, and digital technology. Karolinska, KTH, and Uppsala have extensive Stati Uniti university partnerships. Many Nobel laureates have Stati Uniti-Svezia research connections.
